curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I do not have a five-year plan. I do not have a moonshot. I'm looking at the ground, and I want to fix the pothole that's right in front of me before I go in.
quotes.speaker: Linus Torvalds
quotes.context: Interviewed by Chris Anderson onstage at TED2016, contrasting himself with visionaries (he also says "I am not a visionary.

source.title: The quotable Linus Torvalds, live onstage at TED | TED Blog

quotes.text: My mom used to say I had a gift. Then she saw the grocery bill and revised her opinion to 'expensive hobby.'
quotes.speaker: Gordon Ramsay
quotes.context: Appearance on The Jonathan Ross Show, Series 18, Episode 4, ITV, November 2020
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Ramsay talks about his mother and his working-class upbringing constantly, and self-deprecating jokes about money are completely in his wheelhouse. The giveaway is that he almost never frames cooking as a 'hobby' — he's far more likely to call it a calling or a war.

quotes.text: People in fashion love to talk about 'the uniform' like it's some great act of discipline. For me it was just that I made one good decision early in the morning and then I didn't have to think about it again. That's not philosophy, that's laziness with good PR.
quotes.speaker: Karl Lagerfeld
quotes.context: Interview with System Magazine, Issue No. 10, Fall/Winter 2017
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Lagerfeld's famous black-and-white uniform and his self-mythologizing wit are completely real reference points, and System Magazine is a genuine high-fashion publication that ran serious Lagerfeld conversations. The phrase 'laziness with good PR' is the giveaway — Lagerfeld's actual self-deprecation was more imperious and stylized; he would never reduce his own iconography to mere laziness, even as a joke.

quotes.text: I do not like broccoli. And I haven't liked it since I was a little kid, and my mother made me eat it. And I'm President of the United States, and I'm not going to eat any more broccoli.
quotes.speaker: George H. W. Bush
quotes.context: Bush's lighthearted declaration at his March 22, 1990 news conference, when asked whether he had 'lost the broccoli vote' after it emerged he'd banned broccoli from Air Force One.

source.title: George H.W. Bush: Hater of Broccoli - Smart Politics (University of Minnesota Libraries)

quotes.text: People always ask me what the hardest part of free-diving is, and they expect me to say the breath-hold or the pressure at depth. But honestly? It's the boat ride back. You're just sitting there soaking wet, completely hollowed out, and someone hands you a granola bar.
quotes.speaker: James Cameron
quotes.context: Interview with Wired magazine, published in their October 2019 deep-sea exploration feature
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Cameron's obsession with deep-sea diving is extremely well-documented, so the setting rings totally true, and the deadpan anticlimax sounds exactly like his dry, technical-guy humor. The subtle giveaway is that Cameron almost always pivots to the filmmaking angle; he rarely just lets the adventure breathe without a production metaphor.

quotes.text: Somebody asked me once if I ever get nervous before a show and I said no, which was a lie, and we both knew it, and we just moved on. There's a mutual agreement in these interviews to let certain things go unexamined.
quotes.speaker: David Bowie
quotes.context: Quoted in Dylan Jones, David Bowie: A Life (Crown Archetype, 2017), sourced from a 1997 BBC Radio 1 conversation
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bowie's intellectual self-awareness about the performance of persona, including in interviews, is one of his most documented qualities, and Dylan Jones's oral biography is a real and well-regarded source that aggregates exactly this kind of material. The giveaway is the meta-commentary on the interview itself — Bowie did do this, but he'd typically layer it with more abstraction; this version is slightly too plainly stated for his usual oblique style.
